description: A Next.js website that embeds a pi agent for open-ended English listening corpus design and adds a Kokoro-FastAPI TTS tool for in-browser playback.

userStories.title: Project foundation and session skeleton
userStories.description: As a learner, I want a stable web app foundation and a persistent pi conversation session, so that I can start talking to the agent immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: Next.js App Router project has base metadata, shell UI, and required dependencies
Server-side session creation allocates a unique sessionId and isolated workspace
Create-session and chat API skeletons return usable responses
Session workspace and app data directories are isolated for the agent
Typecheck / lint passes
userStories.notes: Implemented with Codex: added pi SDK server foundation, isolated session workspaces, Node route handlers for session/chat, and a homepage status shell. Validated with lint, typecheck, and build.

userStories.title: Pi agent and Kokoro TTS tool
userStories.description: As a learner, I want the embedded pi agent to synthesize selected corpus into speech, so that I can practice listening directly in the website.
acceptanceCriteria: Server uses createAgentSession() and keeps pi default coding tools
A synthesize_speech custom tool calls Kokoro-FastAPI speech endpoint
Generated audio is stored and returned with playable metadata
Chat API returns both assistant text and newly generated audio items
Kokoro configuration and request failures show friendly errors
Typecheck / lint passes

userStories.title: Listening practice chat UI
userStories.description: As a learner, I want an intuitive chat interface with audio cards, so that I can discuss corpus and listen to the generated material in one place.
acceptanceCriteria: Homepage includes value proposition, chat history, composer, and loading feedback
Frontend can create a session, send messages, and render user/assistant conversation
Frontend renders agent-generated audio cards with in-browser playback
Voice selector and prompt suggestions help users start quickly
Verify in browser
Typecheck / lint passes

userStories.title: Developer setup documentation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want clear setup documentation, so that I can run the site with pi auth and Kokoro-FastAPI quickly.
acceptanceCriteria: README explains project goal, stack, env vars, and startup steps
A .env.example documents pi and Kokoro configuration
Local Kokoro-FastAPI integration guidance includes a docker example
Current limitations and future extensions are documented
Typecheck / lint passes
